keyboardHelp.rotateSkyDescription: Arrow keys rotate the camera. Drag with the mouse for the same free rotation.
keyboardHelp.rotateAboutZenithDescription: Alt plus arrow keys spins the view about the vertical axis only. Matches Alt-drag.
keyboardHelp.advanceSiderealTimeDescription: Ctrl plus left or right arrow advances or rewinds sidereal time. Matches Ctrl-drag.
keyboardHelp.skyViewHelp: Arrow keys rotate the camera. Alt plus arrows rotate about zenith. Ctrl plus left or right advances sidereal time.
keyboardHelp.moveSunDescription: Left and right arrow keys move the Sun along its daily path by 15 minutes.
keyboardHelp.orbitEarthDescription: Arrow keys advance or rewind one solar day. Shift plus arrows advance or rewind one sidereal day.
keyboardHelp.analogClockHandsDescription: Arrow keys nudge the focused clock hand by five minutes.

siderealSolarTime.solarTimeDescription: ordinary clock time, based on the position of the Sun in the sky
siderealSolarTime.siderealTimeDescription: "astronomer's time", based on the right ascension of a star on the observer's meridian

zodiacScreen.latitudeNote: View from latitude 41° N, looking south

screenSummary.playArea: The play area shows a horizon diagram with the Sun's daily path across the sky for a chosen latitude and date.
screenSummary.controlArea: The control area has a Reset All button that returns the screen to its starting state.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Explore the screen by interacting with its controls.

sunPaths.currentDetails: It is day {{day}} of the year at latitude {{latitude}} degrees. The Sun is at altitude {{altitude}} degrees, azimuth {{azimuth}} degrees.

controls.skyViewHelp: Arrow keys rotate the view; Alt plus arrows rotate about the zenith; Ctrl plus left or right advance time.
controls.sunDiscHelp: Arrow keys move the Sun along its daily path, changing the time of day.
controls.sunDiscDayOfYearHelp: Arrow keys change the day of year along the analemma, keeping the time of day fixed.
controls.calendarStripHelp: Drag the marker to change the date. Dragging past either end wraps to the other side of the year. Arrow keys move one day.
controls.latitudePickerHelp: Drag the marker up or down to set the observer latitude. Arrow keys change latitude by one degree.
controls.worldMapHelp: Drag up or down on the map to set the observer latitude. Arrow keys change latitude by one degree.
controls.clockHelp: A 24-hour clock with hour and minute hands. Drag a hand to set the time of day. Arrow keys change the time by five minutes. Crossing midnight advances or rewinds the date.

screenSummary.playArea: The play area shows Earth rotating and orbiting the Sun, comparing the sidereal day with the solar day.
screenSummary.controlArea: The control area has a Reset All button that returns the screen to its starting state.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Explore the screen by interacting with its controls.

siderealSolarTime.currentDetails: Earth is {{solarDays}} solar days ({{siderealDays}} sidereal days) past the vernal equinox; solar time is {{solarClock}}.

controls.orbitEarthHelp: Drag the Earth globe to change the date; whole solar days snap to orbit positions. Shift-drag snaps to whole sidereal days. Arrow keys move one solar day per press; Shift plus arrows move one sidereal day.
controls.solarAnalogClockHelp: Drag a hand to change solar time. Arrow keys nudge the focused hand by five minutes.
controls.siderealAnalogClockHelp: Drag a hand to change sidereal time. Arrow keys nudge the focused hand by five minutes.

screenSummary.playArea: The play area shows a geocentric celestial sphere with Earth at the center, the Sun on the ecliptic, and the zodiac constellations. Drag to rotate the view; use the day-of-year slider to move through the year.
screenSummary.controlArea: The control area has a Reset All button that returns the screen to its starting state.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Explore the screen by interacting with its controls.

zodiac.currentDetails: The Sun is in {{sign}} on {{month}} {{day}}. It is {{clockTime}} local solar time.

preferences.title: Simulation
preferences.defaultLatitudeDescription: Starting latitude for the Sun Paths screen.
